content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Added #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] to both StartJournalDetails (fs.rs:2280) and CompletionJournalDetails (fs.rs:2328) to provide runtime defense-in-depth against field drift. If the model-layer render functions ever emit a field that the adapter-layer parsing struct does not declare, deserialization will now fail immediately with a clear error instead of silently discarding the unrecognized field. This complements the existing round-trip CI tests by catching drift at runtime in production, not just during test execution.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Added #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] attribute to StartJournalDetails struct at src/adapters/fs.rs:2280, immediately below the existing #[derive(Clone, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)] line
2. [Completed] Added #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] attribute to CompletionJournalDetails struct at src/adapters/fs.rs:2328, immediately below the existing #[derive(Clone, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)] line
3. [Completed] Ran cargo test (683 unit + 351 lib + 128 integration + 1 doc = 1163 total, 0 failed), cargo clippy -- -D warnings (clean), cargo fmt --check on src/adapters/fs.rs (clean)

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 683 unit tests passed, 0 failed; 1163 total across all test targets, 0 failed
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings: no warnings or errors
- cargo fmt --check on src/adapters/fs.rs: clean (only pre-existing formatting diffs in unrelated tests/unit/agent_execution_test.rs)
- Grep confirms #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] present on StartJournalDetails at line 2280 and CompletionJournalDetails at line 2328
- Existing round-trip tests start_journal_details_round_trips_with_model_render and completion_journal_details_round_trips_with_model_render continue to pass, confirming the deny_unknown_fields attribute does not reject the current field set
- Forward-compatibility note: deny_unknown_fields is appropriate here because these structs are internal adapter-layer types that must stay in sync with model.rs render functions — they are not public API and do not need to tolerate unknown fields from external sources

## Outstanding Risks

- Pre-existing formatting drift in tests/unit/agent_execution_test.rs is unrelated to these changes but will appear in cargo fmt --check across the full workspace.
